title: Japanese firm launches hyper-realistic capsule toy PC parts you can assemble and play with tiny motherboards, cases, and CPUs are coming after Tarlin inks collab with the big four PC parts makers
summary: A Japanese capsule toy maker has announced an official collaboration with ASRock, Gigabyte, MSI, and Intel to make tiny PC components that buyers 'can assemble and play with.'

title: Get an RTX 5060 gaming laptop loaded with Ryzen 7 CPU and 32GB RAM for $1,099 mobile gaming upgrade just got $300 cheaper
summary: The Gigabyte Aero X16 positions itself as a compelling mid-range gaming laptop offering a smooth high-refresh display, capable RTX 5060 graphics performance, and future-ready upgrade options.

title: Apple reportedly lobbies Uncle Sam for access to Chinese memory chips tech giant allegedly wants to buy from blacklisted CXMT
summary: Following a historic price hike, the Financial Times reports that Apple is lobbying in Washington to secure approval to buy cheaper RAM from CXMT. The manufacturer is currently designated as a Chinese military company, but is not entirely banned by the White House.

title: Steam Machine scalping hits $3,000 on eBay as sellers list preorder reservations scalpers already flipping queues for 2X the MSRP of the 2TB model
summary: Several listings for Steam Machine pre-orders are being sold at markups so high that buyers will have to pay 140% to 167% above Valve's selling price.

title: Intel's next-gen 52-core Nova Lake CPU could pull up to 474W high-end LGA1954 motherboards may need three 8-pin power connectors to feed the monster
summary: Intel's flagship 52-core Nova Lake processor could feature a 474W PL2 power limit. At the same time, the new LGA1954 platform may introduce motherboard tiers for up to 175W CPUs and optional triple EPS power connectors on enthusiast boards.

title: Bambu Lab A2L 3D printer review: The A1 grows up
summary: Bambu Lab adds a bigger bed slinger to their lineup.

title: Modded Steam Controller can automatically charge itself like a robot vacuum enthusiast creates GitHub program that uses the vibration motor to walk it back to its docking station
summary: Ray Foss built this program that uses computer vision to let your Steam Controller slide back towards its charging puck by just using its built-in haptic motors. You can also try it for yourself by visiting the web app and using a webcam so it can see your controller and charging puck.

title: Arduino launches Modulino Hub and Extender modules to support up to 64 I2C devices, 30-meter range
summary: Arduino has just introduced three new Modulino modules: the Modulino Hub to support up to eight I2C devices (64 devices with 8 Hubs), the Modulino Extender to extend the I2C range to up to 30 meters, and the Modulino Motors to control two DC motors or a single stepper motor with precision. I ll focus on the I2C modules in this post, as I find the Modulino Extender module particularly interesting, since I always considered I2C to be a short-range communication protocol, and never thought of using it for (relatively) long-range wired communication. Modulino Hub While the I2C protocol supports up to 127 devices in theory, address conflicts can become an issue in complex builds where multiple sensors or actuators share the same default address. The Modulino Hub aims to solve that, and multiple Hubs can be daisy-chained, each with its own address, to support up to 64 devices. Modulino Hub specifications: [...] The post Arduino launches Modulino Hub and Extender modules to support up to 64 I2C devices, 30-meter range appeared first on CNX Software - Embedded Systems News .

title: Research at UAB underway on wearables that are thin and durable
summary: A thin network of molybdenum disulfide ribbons is about to enter a proof of concept for durable ultra-thin sensing that could be worn over longer periods on human skin.

title: Best of Sensors: Electro Scan s Solar Drone Wins Ag & Enviro
summary: Solar Drone by Electro Scan inspects and cleans solar arrays using imaging sensors and can navigate mountains and rooftops. The product won Best of Sensors 2026 in the ag and environmental category.

title: PlayStation sales just had its worst May in 25 years, and Xbox's was the worst ever
summary: Market research firm Circana's Mat Piscatella reports that May 2026 saw the lowest number of PlayStation consoles sold in the United States during any May since 2000, a few months before the PlayStation 2's launch. Meanwhile, Xbox unit sales experienced their worst May on record. Read Entire Article

title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 gets staggered release after Trump administration cites national security concerns
summary: Sol, the flagship model in the GPT-5.6 lineup, is built with a robust safety stack with guardrails against higher-risk activities, sensitive cyber requests, and repeated misuse. Terra is designed for balanced reasoning and agentic workloads, with OpenAI claiming that it offers similar performance to GPT-5.5 while being 2x cheaper. Luna... Read Entire Article

title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on any country imposing digital services taxes on US tech firms
summary: Several European countries have been considering or already applying digital services taxes to large online platforms. These surcharges are aimed at revenue generated by activities such as digital advertising and online marketplaces areas dominated by US-based companies. Read Entire Article

title: The Steam Machine is sold out, overpriced, can't quite do 4K and now scalpers are asking $3,000 for it
summary: Only weeks after landing in users' hands, the Steam Controller's unique properties have quickly inspired interesting, or at least amusing, new features.
